title: Nassella pauciciliata
intro: Nassella pauciciliata, also known as Poverty Grass or Poverty Weed, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of various health issues such as fever, cough, and cold due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Nassella pauciciliata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Nassella pauciciliata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
taxonomy: Nassella pauciciliata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Stipeae. The genus Nassella is a group of grasses native to the Americas, and N. pauciciliata is one of its species. Nassella pauciciliata is a type of bunchgrass that is commonly found in mountainous regions. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the most up-to-date taxonomy.
